artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Chiba University research news about generative AI use, but not policy or guidance for student or teaching use.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The page lists agenda entries such as ChatGPT等の生成AIの利用に関する留意事項 and 教育・学習における生成AIの利用に関する指針について, but does not provide the guideline text.
artifacts.notes: Used for discovery context only; not used as claim evidence.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The page describes Chiba University research on generative AI usage patterns, not rules or guidance for Chiba University students or instructors.
artifacts.notes: Rejected because research output does not establish institutional AI-use policy.

methodsAttempted.query: site:chiba-u.ac.jp "ChatGPT等の生成AIの利用に関する留意事項"
methodsAttempted.notes: Found official council agenda and minutes; rejected for claim evidence because they only record that the topic was reported.
Followed the official Chiba University academics page link to the public guideline PDF in Google Drive.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery located Chiba University's central academics page linking to a public guideline PDF on generative AI use in teaching and learning. Council agenda/minutes and research-news sources were rejected for claim extraction because they lacked substantive policy instructions.

targets.sourceTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
expectedThemes: generative AI in teaching and learning
instructor discretion to restrict AI use
fairness when AI use is allowed

stopConditions: Stop after the officially linked guideline PDF is fetched and citation-ready snippets are extracted.
Do not use research-news pages as policy evidence.
Do not infer more than the teaching-and-learning guideline states.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針

artifacts.claimText: Chiba University's teaching-and-learning guideline says the university does not uniformly prohibit generative AI use in teaching and learning and promotes learning about, with, and aside from generative AI according to purpose.

citation.citationTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Chiba University's guideline says each course instructor judges whether generative AI use is consistent with class objectives and may prohibit or restrict generative AI use where it would significantly undermine achievement of educational objectives.

citation.citationTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Chiba University's guideline says that when instructors prohibit or restrict generative AI use in a class, they should clearly state that in the syllabus or other course handouts and explain the reason to students.

citation.citationTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Chiba University's guideline tells students not to enter personal information or confidential information into generative AI and notes the information-leakage risk of entering unpublished research plans or research results.

citation.citationTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Chiba University's guideline says that when instructors allow students to use generative AI in class, instructors should consider fairness because outputs may differ depending on external-service registration and paid or free versions.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 千葉大学は生成 AI の教育・学習での利用を一律に禁止することはせず、その目的に応じて、「生成 AI についての学び」「生成 AI を用いた学び」「生成 AI によらない学び」をそれぞれ推進する。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Chiba University does not uniformly prohibit generative AI use in teaching and learning and promotes learning about, with, and aside from generative AI according to purpose.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 生成 AI の授業での利用は、それが授業の目的に合致することが前提であり、合致するかどうかは、それぞれの授業の担当教員が判断する。生成 AI の利用によって、教育目標の達成が大きく損なわれる授業においては、教員は受講生に生成 AI の利用を禁止・制限することができる。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Generative AI use in class must align with class objectives; each instructor judges alignment and may prohibit or restrict use where it would significantly undermine educational objectives.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 授業において生成 AI の利用を禁止・制限する際には、教員はその旨を当該授業のシラバス等に明記する。また、なぜ利用を禁止・制限するのかを受講生にわかりやすく説明することが望まれる。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: When prohibiting or restricting generative AI use in class, instructors should state that in the syllabus or similar materials and explain the reason clearly to students.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 学生は、生成 AI を教育・学習で利用する際には、以下の諸点について留意する。生成 AI には個人情報や機密情報は入力しないこと（公開されても差し支えない情報のみ入力すること）。生成 AI に未公表の研究計画や研究成果などを入力すると、情報漏洩につながる危険性があること。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students should keep these points in mind: do not enter personal or confidential information into generative AI; entering unpublished research plans or results may lead to information leakage.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 授業において学生に生成 AI の利用を認める場合には、外部サービス利用登録の有無、有料版か無料版かなどにより、成果物等に差が生まれ得るため、教員は公平性に配慮する。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: When allowing students to use generative AI in class, instructors should consider fairness because differences may arise depending on service registration or paid/free versions.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 千葉大学の教育・学習における生成 AI の利用についての指針
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a teaching-and-learning guideline claim; source is officially linked by Chiba University and the claim follows the original wording.
Approved because guideline item 2 explicitly gives course instructors discretion to prohibit or restrict use in the stated condition.
Approved with 'should' wording because the original Japanese says 明記する and 望まれる; claim avoids overstating beyond the source.
Approved as a privacy/data-handling claim because the original says students should keep the points in mind and specifically says not to enter personal or confidential information.
Approved because guideline item 6 directly instructs instructors to consider fairness when generative AI is allowed.

artifacts.title: Chiba University generative AI teaching and learning guidance evidence snapshot
artifacts.summary: Chiba University's officially linked teaching-and-learning guideline says generative AI is not uniformly prohibited, gives instructors discretion to restrict use when educational objectives would be undermined, asks instructors to state restrictions in syllabi or similar materials, cautions students not to enter personal or confidential information, and asks instructors to consider fairness when AI use is allowed.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.

The guideline PDF is hosted on Google Drive but was discovered through and linked from Chiba University's official academics page; the staging record preserves both the official page and the fetched PDF source.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
